title: So um liquid breathing right
wikitext:
[]: So, um, liquid breathing, right?             Quote Post by Nightweaver20xx » Wed Nov 24, 2010 10:34 am I just had kind of a revelation today after I woke up in bed. The pilots all breathe LCL, right? Well, considering that's liquid breathing, and that liquid fills your lungs and windpipe with fluid, and that you need air to pass past your larynx in order to talk... How the hell can the pilots talk in their entry plugs?

[]: Re: So, um, liquid breathing, right?             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Wed Nov 24, 2010 10:50 am Nightweaver20xx wrote: I just had kind of a revelation today after I woke up in bed. The pilots all breathe LCL, right? Well, considering that's liquid breathing, and that liquid fills your lungs and windpipe with fluid, and that you need air to pass past your larynx in order to talk... How the hell can the pilots talk in their entry plugs?

[]: Re: So, um, liquid breathing, right?             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Nov 24, 2010 11:00 am Nightweaver20xx wrote: How the hell can the pilots talk in their entry plugs?

[]: Re: So, um, liquid breathing, right?             Quote Post by skikes » Wed Nov 24, 2010 11:14 am Nightweaver20xx wrote: How the hell can the pilots talk in their entry plugs?

[]: Re: So, um, liquid breathing, right?             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Wed Nov 24, 2010 11:58 am skikes wrote: Lets just pretend that when the Eva are in battle mode an electromagnetic wave (or some such) makes the LCL act like a gas and the times it doesn't... I dunno.

[]:             Quote Post by Assasinate » Fri Nov 26, 2010 7:44 am from what you've said... ive also noticed that.... but well i dunno why the artist forgot about that its not allowed to talk while your body is being filled up w/ liquid... or they just did it for fun

[]:             Quote Post by Rodeo » Fri Nov 26, 2010 3:51 pm There is no sound in space.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat Nov 27, 2010 11:42 am This was done before in "The Abyss" but no one talked when they had the special liquid. In fact, the characters in "Evangelion" shouldn't be talking at all with LCL. (A possible mind-reader would be a good replacement. They do have the head bands on them, so why not make another good reason to make these handy?)

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sat Nov 27, 2010 12:37 pm tomrule123 wrote: This was done before in "The Abyss" but no one talked when they had the special liquid. In fact, the characters in "Evangelion" shouldn't be talking at all with LCL. (A possible mind-reader would be a good replacement. They do have the head bands on them, so why not make another good reason to make these handy?)

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Nov 27, 2010 2:05 pm Well, sound is some sort of fluctuation in the pressure of the medium it travels through, like some sort of wave, if it can travel tough metal and bone (which it does), it should be able to travel through LCL as well. Babies in their mother's belly are able to hear a lot in the last trimester, to the point of recognizing the opening of the mother's favourite TV show even if they're completely enveloped by a liquid medium the whole time.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Nov 27, 2010 3:15 pm Sound travels just fine through liquid -- in fact the speed of sound underwater is much faster than in air (about 5 times as fast) The difficulty is with speaking -- the vocal cords won't vibrate as fast, or as much, for the same amount of effort. The pilots would need a mechanical larynx -- such as used by people who have had a laryngeotomy -- to take over that job.

[]: Re: So, um, liquid breathing, right?             Quote Post by nomis1242 » Sat Nov 27, 2010 5:49 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Ah yes. The LCL "phase shift". That's a fun topic to argue about.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sat Nov 27, 2010 7:48 pm My bro just told me that maybe the cameras could read the labial of the pilots and a speech synthesizer will reproduce the pilots voices. It seems plausible.

[]: Re: So, um, liquid breathing, right?             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Sat Nov 27, 2010 11:30 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Ah yes. The LCL "phase shift". That's a fun topic to argue about. It's fuzzy scientifically. Which is why I like to point out the fact that you are watching a cartoon.

[]:             Quote Post by Zan » Sat Nov 27, 2010 11:30 pm As with many things in Evangelion, it's part science, part pure magic. The extent to which it is either is left up to the viewer

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Nov 28, 2010 3:18 am Zan wrote: As with many things in Evangelion, it's part science, part sufficiently advanced technology. The extent to which it is either is left up to the viewer

[]:             Quote Post by Galaxy News Reidio » Wed Dec 01, 2010 7:50 am No, I think "magic" was right first time. How else do you explain half of what goes down?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Dec 01, 2010 7:56 am Galaxy News Reidio wrote: How else do you explain half of what goes down?

[]:             Quote Post by Galaxy News Reidio » Wed Dec 01, 2010 8:01 am I know about that, but when you have sanity-blasting monsters from outside mortal ken I don't think science quite cuts it anymore.

[]:             Quote Post by Zan » Wed Dec 01, 2010 11:12 am Clark's Third Law: Any sufficiently advanced technology is indistinguishable from magic
summary:

The pilots all breathe LCL, right? Well, considering that's liquid breathing, and that liquid fills your lungs and windpipe with fluid, and that you need air to pass past your larynx in order to talk... How the hell can the pilots talk in their entry plugs?

[]: Re: So,
